reviews.review_description:
The games fun, it has solid realistic combat and weapons that actually feel grounded instead of plastic toys.

But the blocking system is basically a joke. It’s too easy to abuse, and enemies weirdly turn into unkillable statues the moment they swing. Kicking is also clunky and unintuitive, like it was added as an afterthought and never touched again.

Early access explains some of it, sure, but it doesn’t really excuse how empty it feels content-wise. There’s not much to actually do once the novelty wears off.

What it really needs going forward is modes that are maps with usable tools, and enemies that don’t immediately jump you like they’ve been waiting their whole life for this one moment.

developer_response.body: Hey 03v45, thanks for the constructive criticism- J

reviews.review_description: First off, the unarmed combat in this game and how good it feels is AMAZING. The kicks are a bit jank, but other than that this game gives striking the solidness and weight that many VR gamex lack. Enemies actually REACT and stumble when getting rocked in the head that feels satisfying but also realistic enough to where you don't feel like One Punch Man. The implementation of grappling techniques are also great and very unique. Of course since this game is still being developed, so the lack of much content is not a huge blemish, but is a blemish nonetheless. This game has a lot of potential and I'm excited to see updates!

The fist fighting is pretty good, reminds me of the Arkham games but I do wish that some of the enemies in the rogue like mode would chill out and stop swinging like the flash. I really hope the dev/ devs working on this game could make the fighting a bit easier, for the rogue like mode maybe try adding a difficulty system maybe. I would also like to see a few new moves. Other than that the game is really good

developer_response.body:
Hi Snake,
Thanks for the review! Really glad you’re enjoying the combat, and the Arkham comparison means a lot.

That feedback on rogue-like enemy speed is super helpful. We’re still tuning the pacing, enemy aggression, and overall difficulty so fights feel intense without feeling unfair. A difficulty system is a great suggestion, especially for rogue-like mode, and we’ll definitely keep that in mind as we keep improving the game.

New moves are also something we want to keep expanding over time. Appreciate you playing and helping shape Haymaker as it develops.

reviews.review_title: A stand-out for combat in vr
reviews.review_description:
More of a 4.5/5
I've been playing this game almost daily for 2-3 months now it's just that addictive, while there is no story to mention just standing toe to toe with the enemies being able to dodge and counter attack in a way that feels mostly realistic is awesome. While a story mode or a multiplayer fight mode would be cool building out levels and giving a bit more depth to the weapons and their ability to connect with your opponent more frequently would be nice.

developer_response.body:
Hi Jake!
Thank you so much for playing almost daily. That means a lot.
We’re glad the dodging, countering, and close-up fights feel good. Story mode, bigger levels, and better weapon connection are all great feedback. We’ll keep building Haymaker out and improving the depth as development continues.

developer_response.body:

More realistic blood, new moves, and continued physics improvements are all things we’re looking at as the game grows. Appreciate the feedback and support.

reviews.review_title: A game for hand to hand brawlers.
reviews.review_description: I really do love this game how creative and intuitive the mechanics are to this game especially with the kicking, the more time I've spent in the training gym to learn the more I put it to action and find it so satisfying how I you fight and land your attacks. For me personally I really enjoy what this games offers, I know ain't much to do and I can understand to my knowledge people want more content to do like going through some maps as a arcade beat em up which is nothing wrong with that. I made the title of my review the way it is because I feel that what this game tries to do, to make a hand to hand sandbox brawler for the type of people who just want a brawler type of vr game. Yeah someone can make a good argument about how other physics based sandbox games have hand to hand combat like Blade and sorcery, Battle Talent, Nightclub simulator, Drunken bar fight etc, But none of those games really capture the immersion of having a true back ally brawl against some npc punks utilizing all of the games mechanics and fundamentals that other vr titles just don't have. So concluding this is give it full 5 stars because I am someone who genuinely love this game for what it is, I can only recommend this for those people who do want that full immersive back ally brawler experience.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the thoughtful review! We’re really glad the training gym, kicking, physics, and hand-to-hand mechanics are clicking for you.

Haymaker is built for that immersive back alley brawler feel, so it means a lot that you see what we’re going for. More content is coming as the game grows.

reviews.review_description: Out of all of my boxing/fighting games I always looked for a realistic knockout, but some games can’t give that, maybe because the game only has animated knockdowns or the whole body goes limp, but on this game the body doesn’t go limp and they don’t have animated knockdowns(at least I don’t think so). I have a lot more to say still and may finish the rest of my review later.

developer_response.body: Cjj! I made this game with a similar mindset about KOs- I have no KO animations other than the ones when you hit a guy in the gut and they double over to a half-downed state. I have worked on this for 5 years and a lot of that was getting the KOs to look good and feel right (also a lot of time spent on getting the bots to fight each other well). I much appreciate the review and look forward to whatever else you add!

reviews.review_title: Game contains demonic #666 on all stats.
reviews.review_description: I hate that this game is fun but I cannot play it with this demonic number being used repeatedly on the stats percentages. I've seen the number back to back like its purposely supposed to be displayed. PLEASE CHANGE OR REMOVE THIS. I'll remove this review and write a new one once you've changed it. Please make the game appropriate for everyone.

developer_response.body: The value you’re referencing (0.666…) is simply the result of standard math (2/3), which appears naturally in calculations throughout the game. There’s no symbolic or intentional meaning behind it, and no plan to alter core math-based systems.

reviews.review_description: There's more here than meets the eye! It's not really a game campaign. It's more like a contest of who can last the longest. The setup is simple, but the fighting is very detailed. It's just fun and satisfying for fighting sim lovers. Throwing the hands is fun, but learning that arsenal of moves, the grappling, the knees, etc, makes it more in-depth! You really have to play it through to see how detailed it is. If you love to fight in sanboxes, Haymaker is definitely for you!  There's so much potential to make this game greater than 5 stars!

developer_response.body: Many thanks, LordPower! So much more to do, indeed! Join the discord and help shape things if you wish. Hoping to do more than the current modes soon.

Notes taken on girl combatants and the NPC doing his NPC things. Hop in the Discord if you want to give more feedback!

reviews.review_title: DO NOT SLEEP ON THIS GAME
reviews.review_description: Quite possibly one of the grittiest fighting games I’ve had the chance to play yet on the Meta quest. ￼ Haymaker captures that underground fight club feel. And with the joint physics system allows you to execute incredible arm, brakes, and takedowns. You can find my full review on YouTube. Look for PR-IN-VR. ￼￼

developer_response.body: Thanks PR-IN-VR! Much more to come- do stay tuned!

reviews.review_title: Peak vr Gaming but I have 1 problem...
reviews.review_description:
The Game is genuinely PEAK. i love the punching and bone breaking and all of that but my one problem with the game is that every time i join the game i am forced to do the tutorial and it is just soooooooooooo anoying having to listen to Some guy yap about how the game works even though i already have mastered every single skill in the game AND THE DIALOG IS UNSKIPPABLE!! please just add if you do the tut once you don't have to do it again. 
9/10 overall

developer_response.body: Hey Gorilla! I'm trying to figure out this bug- it's ridiculous. Can you hop in the discord and @wimtar me? For the mean time you can click on the NEWS title text on the home menu to skip the tutorial requirement.

reviews.review_description: imagine how fun it would be to be doing 2v2s or 1v1s with friends and doing waves together not saying the game is bad im saying something that would make the game better!

reviews.review_title: Best hand to hand combat on Quest 3
reviews.review_description: The moment I witnessed my first KO was the moment I realized this is the best hand to hand combat game on Meta Quest 3, there's nothing like it. I don't know what's better - the visceral feeling you get when landing a knockout punch or the dopamine rush when you manage a clean counter. One thing is certain - this game will keep you entertained for a long time. Also the developer is constantly adding new features and talking to his fan base which is always a great sign. Highly recommend.

developer_response.body: Thank you Ravnash! This is a great review <3

reviews.review_title: One of the best fighting games 🔥
reviews.review_description: If you are thinking on buying this game do it it has realistic knockouts and reactions and I just hope you guys make more games like this and if could you guys make more fighting skills if you guys have more time but this is the best game
The best game i've ever played

Good game but you should add better and more realistic ragdolls and better gore and you should also add guns and have the npc's surrender so you can knock them out by hitting them on the head and you should also add more maps and npc's and weapons thank you for reading g and please haymaker lords add these things please!!!!!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback and review GoofyLynx! I'll keep these noted!

reviews.review_title: Probably one of the most fun games you c
reviews.review_description:
This game is great. Especially if you love  punching / boxing in VR.

￼ But for the love of all that is holy get rid of the retard guard mission in rogue mode
i think there should be a story mode and we can make our own character and add multiplayer this game is still good but can upper the graphics but still a great game.👍
Beats drunken bar fight and nightclub simulator together. I love how you can break people’s hands if you grab their punch and all of the moves are great. Please add guns

developer_response.body: Thanks Maxi! I've got a revolver in the experimental build channel- directions to get there are in the Discord if you need them.
